title: What works for whom? What theories may predict about the effectiveness of measures against employment discrimination
summary_en: Theory-led review asking which classes of anti-discrimination measures should work for which target groups. Distinguishes interventions targeted at employer perception (audit/testing, training) from those targeted at the employment process (blind CVs, structured interviews). Conclusion: which measure works depends on whether the discrimination is taste-based, statistical, or driven by network effects.
policy_relevance: Actionable framework for the Hungarian Equal Treatment Authority and EU equality bodies: the 'best' anti-discrimination intervention is conditional on the kind of discrimination. Helpful when designing pilot programmes. Caveat: theoretical synthesis with selective empirical evidence; mechanism identification on Hungarian data is rare.
